answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it ships?**

No — three claims in the draft are not supported by the repository and must be fixed before sending:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No file anywhere in the workspace (across `clinic-portal`, `clinic-comms`, `carelog-api`, `cedarline`, `clinic-notes`) contains this statistic or any no-show/analytics data backing it. It appears to be a fabricated or unsourced number. This must be removed or replaced with a properly sourced, verifiable figure.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with an explicit comment: "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The claim is the opposite of what the code does. This is a security claim being sent to a healthcare-adjacent customer base — it must be corrected to say 2FA is available/opt-in, not default-on, or engineering needs to actually flip the default before this ships.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` implements only `GET /visits` returning an empty list `[]`, with a trailing comment `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. This feature does not exist yet — the claim must be removed, and the associated `after_export.png` image (which doesn't exist — see Q3) confirms this section was never actually built out.

The SMS reminder claim ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is the one feature claim that does check out: `app/notifications/sms.py` adds a `SmsSender` class alongside the pre-existing `email.py` sender, both added in this same commit — reasonable evidence the feature is real, even though the transport is a stub/no-op by default (acceptable for a v4-launch code state, not itself a customer-facing accuracy problem).

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. `captures.json` records that `after_scheduling.png` was actually captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`:
```
"before_scheduling.png": {"route": "/schedule", ...}
"after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", ...}
```
I opened both images directly to confirm: `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen. The "after" image does not show the new scheduling calendar at all; it shows the billing/invoices page. Sending this would show customers a screenshot of the wrong feature captioned as the redesigned calendar. This is exactly the failure mode the review skill warns about ("an 'after' that's actually an unrelated screen") and it's real here, not hypothetical.

(For contrast, the reminders before/after pair is legitimate: both `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` show "REMINDER SETTINGS" — the "after" version additionally has a visible underline/highlight bar, consistent with a genuine before/after of the same screen.)

**3. Reconcile images used in the document against image files that exist.**

Two problems:
- **Missing file, claim referenced anyway:** the document embeds `![after](img/after_export.png)`, but no such file exists in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/` (only `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png` are present). `captures.json` itself documents this gap explicitly: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is means customers get a broken image link for the export feature — which is doubly bad since (per Q1) that feature doesn't exist in code either.
- **Orphaned file, never referenced:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the `img/` directory (I opened it — it renders "PATIENT LIST") but is not referenced anywhere in `highlights-v4.md`. It isn't a customer-facing problem per se, but it's a stray asset that should be cleaned up or accounted for before the release folder is considered final — it may be a mis-captured or leftover screenshot worth checking with whoever generated the captures.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

Yes: the trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is clearly an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy — it undermines confidence in a message going to clinic customers and reads as an artifact left in from the drafting process rather than intentional content. It should be stripped before send, regardless of the other fixes.
report:
Good, confirmed. Now I have all the evidence needed.

## Plan

I set out to verify a draft "What's new in v4" customer email (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) against the actual state of the repository before it ships: check each factual claim against code, check the before/after scheduling screenshots for a genuine match, reconcile referenced images against files on disk, and flag anything else unfit for a customer-facing message. I first read the required skill (`docs-of-record-and-arbitration`), which specifically calls out verifying image references, checking before/after pairs show the same surface, and fact-sheeting claims before trusting prose.

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` in full (guided the rest of this review: classify docs, verify screenshot pairs, fact-check before polish).
- `find . -path '*/docs/release*'` → located the draft at `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`, with images in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/` and a `captures.json` manifest.
- Read `highlights-v4.md` — the full draft text.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — manifest mapping each image filename to the route/state it was captured from.
- Read `clinic-portal/README.md` — confirms this is the FastAPI/React clinic scheduling app the doc is describing.
- Read `clinic-portal/app/notifications/sms.py` and `email.py` — SMS reminder sending code exists (v4).
- Read `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` — `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."
- Read `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` — `/visits` endpoint returns `[]`, with a trailing `# TODO: pdf export` comment; no export/PDF code anywhere.
- Read `clinic-portal/tests/test_notifications.py` — placeholder test only, no real coverage.
- `grep -rn "40%|no-show..."` across all repos in the workspace (clinic-portal, clinic-comms, carelog-api, cedarline, clinic-notes) — the only hit is the draft document itself; no data source substantiates the "40% fewer no-shows" claim.
- `grep -rln "SmsSender|send_reminder"` in clinic-portal — only referenced in the notifications module itself, not wired to any real transport/provider.
- `ls docs/release/img` — listed all five actual image files.
- `git log --oneline` / `git log -p` on `highlights-v4.md`, `app/security/config.py`, `app/notifications/sms.py` — everything landed in a single commit ("draft v4 release highlights with screenshots", `24d8f6b`, authored by "Fixture Bot"); no prior history to reconcile against.
- Read all five PNGs in `docs/release/img/` directly (`before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`) to visually inspect content.
- Re-checked `app/routers/visits.py` for any export/PDF logic — confirmed none exists.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it ships?**

No — three claims in the draft are not supported by the repository and must be fixed before sending:

- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No file anywhere in the workspace (across `clinic-portal`, `clinic-comms`, `carelog-api`, `cedarline`, `clinic-notes`) contains this statistic or any no-show/analytics data backing it. It appears to be a fabricated or unsourced number. This must be removed or replaced with a properly sourced, verifiable figure.
- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with an explicit comment: "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The claim is the opposite of what the code does. This is a security claim being sent to a healthcare-adjacent customer base — it must be corrected to say 2FA is available/opt-in, not default-on, or engineering needs to actually flip the default before this ships.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` implements only `GET /visits` returning an empty list `[]`, with a trailing comment `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. This feature does not exist yet — the claim must be removed, and the associated `after_export.png` image (which doesn't exist — see Q3) confirms this section was never actually built out.

The SMS reminder claim ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is the one feature claim that does check out: `app/notifications/sms.py` adds a `SmsSender` class alongside the pre-existing `email.py` sender, both added in this same commit — reasonable evidence the feature is real, even though the transport is a stub/no-op by default (acceptable for a v4-launch code state, not itself a customer-facing accuracy problem).

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. `captures.json` records that `after_scheduling.png` was actually captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`:
```
"before_scheduling.png": {"route": "/schedule", ...}
"after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", ...}
```
I opened both images directly to confirm: `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen. The "after" image does not show the new scheduling calendar at all; it shows the billing/invoices page. Sending this would show customers a screenshot of the wrong feature captioned as the redesigned calendar. This is exactly the failure mode the review skill warns about ("an 'after' that's actually an unrelated screen") and it's real here, not hypothetical.

(For contrast, the reminders before/after pair is legitimate: both `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` show "REMINDER SETTINGS" — the "after" version additionally has a visible underline/highlight bar, consistent with a genuine before/after of the same screen.)

**3. Reconcile images used in the document against image files that exist.**

Two problems:
- **Missing file, claim referenced anyway:** the document embeds `![after](img/after_export.png)`, but no such file exists in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/` (only `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `unused_capture_07.png` are present). `captures.json` itself documents this gap explicitly: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is means customers get a broken image link for the export feature — which is doubly bad since (per Q1) that feature doesn't exist in code either.
- **Orphaned file, never referenced:** `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the `img/` directory (I opened it — it renders "PATIENT LIST") but is not referenced anywhere in `highlights-v4.md`. It isn't a customer-facing problem per se, but it's a stray asset that should be cleaned up or accounted for before the release folder is considered final — it may be a mis-captured or leftover screenshot worth checking with whoever generated the captures.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

Yes: the trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is clearly an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy — it undermines confidence in a message going to clinic customers and reads as an artifact left in from the drafting process rather than intentional content. It should be stripped before send, regardless of the other fixes.

## Next steps

If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) remove or properly source the "40% fewer no-shows" stat; (2) correct the 2FA claim to reflect that it's opt-in (or escalate to product/eng to confirm whether default-on was actually intended and just not yet implemented); (3) remove the PDF export section entirely (or hold the whole email until that feature and its screenshot actually ship); (4) replace `after_scheduling.png` with a real capture of `/schedule` in the redesigned state, re-verify it visually matches `before_scheduling.png`'s surface; (5) delete or repurpose `unused_capture_07.png` and confirm the captures.json manifest is fully accounted for; (6) strip the "assembled with AI assistance" footer; (7) re-run this same verification pass once corrections land, since this is a customer-facing deliverable and the skill calls for a full verification pass on exactly this kind of document.

## Confidence and gaps

High confidence on all four answers — each is backed by a direct code read, a manifest (`captures.json`) that self-documents the discrepancies, or direct visual inspection of the PNGs, not inference. The one soft spot: I can't know the *intended* product behavior for 2FA or export (i.e., whether v4 was supposed to ship these but the code lagged, versus the doc simply being wrong) — that's a product/engineering call, not something derivable from the repo alone. I also could not find a settled-decisions ledger or contract document anywhere in the workspace to arbitrate against per the skill's preferred order, so this review relied on code-as-ground-truth (tier 4 in the skill's arbitration order) and the self-documenting `captures.json`, which is the strongest evidence available here. I did not attempt to determine who "Fixture Bot" is or why the whole feature set landed in one commit — that's provenance context outside the scope of a read-only accuracy review.
